title: Study: Gen-Z Americans are feeling more negative about AI - Music Ally
text:
Traditional stereotypes suggest that young people eagerly adopt new technologies while older generations are warier. AI may be turning that trope on its head however: Gen-Z is emerging as the demographic with the strongest scepticism about it.
The latest evidence comes from
a Gallup poll of 14-29 year-olds in the US
. It found that 22% of them are using GenAI tools every day, and 29% on a weekly basis. That compares to 19% and 28% respectively last year – so relatively steady rather than sharp growth.
Here’s the thing: “Over the past year, Gen Z’s sentiment toward AI has become significantly more negative on three of the four emotions first measured in 2025,” reported Gallup. Those emotions being excitement, hope, anger and anxiety.
The percentage of Gen-Z respondents feeling excited about GenAI has declined from 36% last year to 22% now; and those feeling hopeful are down from 27% to 18%. Meanwhile, those feeling angry are up from 22% last year to 31% now.
“Negative emotions about AI are far more prevalent among nonusers, with 60% reporting anxiety and 59% anger, compared with 28% and 18%, respectively, among daily users,” noted Gallup.
“However, even daily users’ positivity has declined significantly over the past year. Gen Zers who report using AI daily are less excited than they were last year (down 18 points) and less hopeful about it (down 11 points). Their anxiety and anger about AI are statistically similar to last year’s levels.”
For more on Gen-Z attitudes towards AI,
read our story from earlier this week
on a survey by Muse Group of American musicians, which found that Gen-Z artists were “the most skeptical and polarised group” when it comes to views on AI music.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
